channel.title: NDTV News Search Records Found 1000
channel.description: NDTV.com provides the latest information from and in-depth coverage of India and the world. Find breaking news, India news, top stories, elections, politics, business, cricket, movies, lifestyle, health, live TV, videos, photos and more.

item.title: "Human Rights Disaster": Detainees At ICE's Texas Facility Allege Abuse

media:content.media:description: Camp East Montana at Fort Bliss, where detainees allege abuse by guards

item.description: 90% of detainees interviewed at ICE's Fort Bliss facility said they had either personally been assaulted by the staff or had seen others physically abused, as per a report.
item.content:encoded: 90% of detainees interviewed at ICE's Fort Bliss facility said they had either personally been assaulted by the staff or had seen others physically abused, as per a report.

item.title: Why Europe's Deadly Heatwave Is More Than Just A Summer Crisis

media:content.media:description: The heatwave has now exposed public infrastructure and its incapacity to deal with the sweltering heat.

item.description: A persistent high-pressure system, combined with warmer-than-average sea surface temperatures, has trapped an immense mass of hot air over Europe, driving extreme temperatures across the continent.
item.content:encoded: A persistent high-pressure system, combined with warmer-than-average sea surface temperatures, has trapped an immense mass of hot air over Europe, driving extreme temperatures across the continent.

item.title: US Ropes In Ex-RBI Governor Raghuram Rajan, 2 Other Indians For Key Fed Roles

media:content.media:description: The appointments are part of the five task forces announced by the newly appointed US Federal Reserve

item.description: Rajan will serve on the balance sheet policy task force to examine the costs, benefits, and institutional implications of the Federal Reserve's current balance sheet regime, including its asset holdings and their role in implementing monetary policy.
item.content:encoded: Rajan will serve on the balance sheet policy task force to examine the costs, benefits, and institutional implications of the Federal Reserve's current balance sheet regime, including its asset holdings and their role in implementing monetary policy.

item.title: Thousands Sickened By 'Explosive' Diarrhoea Across US: The Parasite Lurking In Salads

media:content.media:description: Cyclospora outbreak has sickened nearly 7,000 people across 34 US states.

item.description: The microscopic parasite causes severe, often "explosive" diarrhoea that can last for weeks, making it one of the largest foodborne illness outbreaks in recent years.
item.content:encoded: The microscopic parasite causes severe, often "explosive" diarrhoea that can last for weeks, making it one of the largest foodborne illness outbreaks in recent years.

item.title: "Can't Give Up": Trump Denies Removing Immigration Traffic Stops

media:content.media:description: ICE has been under pressure to beef up arrest and deportation numbers.

item.description: It's not clear whether ICE will quickly reverse course and resume most stops, which have been a key tool in Trump's immigration crackdown.
item.content:encoded: It's not clear whether ICE will quickly reverse course and resume most stops, which have been a key tool in Trump's immigration crackdown.

item.title: Security Camera Captures Dog Accidentally Sparking House Fire In US That Killed 3 Pets

media:content.media:description: About 500,000 pets in the US are affected by house fires annually.

item.description: Neighbours managed to rescue Bo and another dog, Addie, from the burning house. However, a third dog, Dakota, and the family's two cats died in the fire.
item.content:encoded: Neighbours managed to rescue Bo and another dog, Addie, from the burning house. However, a third dog, Dakota, and the family's two cats died in the fire.

item.title: Iran Claims It Downed MQ-9 Drone As US Strikes Damage Children's Cancer Hospital

media:content.media:description: A hospital was impacted by the American bombardment in Ahvaz

item.description: IRGC has claimed it has intercepted and destroyed an "enemy" MQ-9 drone over the southwestern city of Andimeshk
item.content:encoded: IRGC has claimed it has intercepted and destroyed an "enemy" MQ-9 drone over the southwestern city of Andimeshk

item.title: Venezuela Earthquake: Death Count Rises To 4,829, Over 20,000 Displaced

media:content.media:description: At least 20,857 people made homeless by the quakes are living in overcrowded camps.

item.description: The 7.2 and 7.5 magnitude quakes struck within a minute of each other on June 24, decimating the coastal state of La Guaira located north of Caracas.
item.content:encoded: The 7.2 and 7.5 magnitude quakes struck within a minute of each other on June 24, decimating the coastal state of La Guaira located north of Caracas.

item.title: Amid War, Trump Says Iran Has Freed Detained US Citizen In Goodwill Gesture
item.description: US President Donald Trump said Wednesday that arch foe Iran has freed an American citizen detained since December 2024 and allowed her to leave the country.
item.content:encoded: US President Donald Trump said Wednesday that arch foe Iran has freed an American citizen detained since December 2024 and allowed her to leave the country.

item.title: Blasts Heard In Kyiv As EU Chief Visits Ukraine Amid Russian Missile Alert

media:content.media:description: European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen is on Ukraine tour to boost defence ties.

item.description: A series of explosions rang out in Kyiv shortly after midnight on Thursday, an AFP journalist said, after a Ukrainian air force alert reported the approach of several ballistic missiles.
item.content:encoded: A series of explosions rang out in Kyiv shortly after midnight on Thursday, an AFP journalist said, after a Ukrainian air force alert reported the approach of several ballistic missiles.

item.title: 3 Policemen Killed, 34 Injured In 2 Terrorist Attacks In Pakistan

media:content.media:description: During the assault, the attackers also set two police vehicles ablaze. (File)

item.description: Terrorists attacked a police convoy in Lower Dir district with hand grenades and automatic weapons on Wednesday, killing three police personnel and injuring 19.
item.content:encoded: Terrorists attacked a police convoy in Lower Dir district with hand grenades and automatic weapons on Wednesday, killing three police personnel and injuring 19.

item.title: JD Vance Says Trump Administration "Screwed Up" Epstein Files Communication

media:content.media:description: JD Vance says Trump administration "absolutely" mishandled the communications surrounding Epstein files.

item.description: During a lengthy podcast interview with Joe Rogan released Wednesday, Vance pointed largely to former Attorney General Pam Bondi, who infamously stated that an alleged "client list" of Epstein's was "sitting on my desk right now."
item.content:encoded: During a lengthy podcast interview with Joe Rogan released Wednesday, Vance pointed largely to former Attorney General Pam Bondi, who infamously stated that an alleged "client list" of Epstein's was "sitting on my desk right now."

item.title: US Military Strikes Ship Trying To Breach Iran Port Blockade

media:content.media:description: It was the first time US has forcibly halted a vessel since reinstating the ports blockade on Tuesday.

item.description: A US aircraft fired on and disabled an unladen oil tanker Wednesday as it tried to break the naval blockade of Iran's ports, the US military said.
item.content:encoded: A US aircraft fired on and disabled an unladen oil tanker Wednesday as it tried to break the naval blockade of Iran's ports, the US military said.

item.title: Ukrainian Drone Kills Chief Engineer At Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Plant: Russia

media:content.media:description: The chief engineer at the Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant has been killed by a Ukrainian drone.

item.description: The chief engineer at the Russian-controlled Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant has been killed by a Ukrainian drone near the station, the head of Russia's state nuclear corporation Rosatom said on Wednesday.
item.content:encoded: The chief engineer at the Russian-controlled Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant has been killed by a Ukrainian drone near the station, the head of Russia's state nuclear corporation Rosatom said on Wednesday.

item.title: Red Fort To Remain Closed Till August 15 For Independence Preparations

media:content.media:description: A senior ASI official said on Wednesday that such orders are issued every year ahead of Independence Day.

item.description: Delhi's iconic Red Fort complex has been closed to the public from July 15 till the 80th Independence Day celebrations on August 15, according to an official order.
item.content:encoded: Delhi's iconic Red Fort complex has been closed to the public from July 15 till the 80th Independence Day celebrations on August 15, according to an official order.

item.title: US Forces Launch New Wave Of Strikes Against Iran
item.description: The United States launched a new wave of strikes against Iran on Wednesday in a bid to curb Tehran's ability to threaten the Strait of Hormuz, the US military said.
item.content:encoded: <iframe title="Player" class="head-story__img img__full lazyload" height="300" scrolling=no frameborder=no src="about:blank" data-src="https://www.ndtv.com/video/embed-player/id/1128184/play-via-app-wap?pWidth=100&pHeight=100&embed_type=story&site=classic&autostart=1"> </iframe>The United States launched a new wave of strikes against Iran on Wednesday in a bid to curb Tehran's ability to threaten the Strait of Hormuz, the US military said.

item.title: Marco Rubio To Chair Global Meet On Far-Left Political Terrorism Today

media:content.media:description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio will chair the meeting on Thursday.

item.description: The US is hosting an international ministerial conference on Thursday to discuss the resurgence of "far-left political terrorism" and joint action to counter the "renewed threat" to society.
item.content:encoded: The US is hosting an international ministerial conference on Thursday to discuss the resurgence of "far-left political terrorism" and joint action to counter the "renewed threat" to society.

item.title: New Port, New Pipelines: Gulf Nations' Billion-Dollar Push To Bypass Hormuz

media:content.media:description: UAE, Iraq have started building major pipeline projects that would move oil around Hormuz

item.description: The goal is simple, to reduce dependence on one of the world's busiest oil routes and limit Tehran's ability to influence global energy supplies. Before the conflict, around one-fifth of the world's oil passed through the strait.
item.content:encoded: The goal is simple, to reduce dependence on one of the world's busiest oil routes and limit Tehran's ability to influence global energy supplies. Before the conflict, around one-fifth of the world's oil passed through the strait.

item.title: Dramatic Video Captures Wildfire Surrounding Freight Train In Canada: "Could Overtake Us Here"

media:content.media:description: CN Rail has temporarily suspended all operations in the affected region.

item.description: The crew members made a narrow escape, and all employees in the area have since been safely evacuated and relocated.
item.content:encoded: The crew members made a narrow escape, and all employees in the area have since been safely evacuated and relocated.

item.title: Trump Wanted '20% Hormuz Toll'. How Gulf Leaders Shut It Down In 24 Hours

media:content.media:description: Officials feared the toll would increase oil and gas prices and create political problems

item.description: According to CNN, US President Donald Trump's sudden announcement of the 20 per cent toll on cargo passing through the Strait of Hormuz prompted a frantic behind-the-scenes push by allies and senior officials to convince the US president to rethink the decision.
item.content:encoded: According to CNN, US President Donald Trump's sudden announcement of the 20 per cent toll on cargo passing through the Strait of Hormuz prompted a frantic behind-the-scenes push by allies and senior officials to convince the US president to rethink the decision.
